description: KOREAN REVOLUTION WILDFIRE JAPS RULE UPHELD AS REFORM BY RELIGIOUS INVESTIGATOR Natives Unfit for SelfGovernment Says Rev E D Soper Talk of SelfDetermination Has Gone to Heads Japan Civilizers But Clumsy Madison X J March 19The revolu tion in Korea was described today as wildfire agitation by a people as yet unfit for selfgovernment by the Rev Dr E D Soper a professor in Drew Theological seminary here who has just returned from a seven months tour of the Far East part of which he spent in Korea Dr Soper was himself born in the Orient the son of a Methodist mis sionary He was of the opinion that notwith standing mistakes iu Japanese rule and abuses of power by some of the Japan ese in the country the Koreans were bet ter off morally physically and econom ically under Japanese control than when they were independent He said the Ko reans were yet children while the Japan ese were adolescents Agitators Stir People Too What the Koerans complained of as misrule he attributed to the inaptitude of the Japanese in handling a big and delicate problem and not to any wilful desire to injure or exploit the Koreans Many
